title: What separates the traders who last from those who blow up — a Sunday thought
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
I've been watching my own trading for a while now.

The losing periods never came from bad analysis.

They came from ignoring what I already knew.

The setup wasn't clean. I entered anyway. The RR wasn't there. I entered anyway. I was bored. I entered anyway.

Discipline isn't a personality trait. It's a system.

The traders I respect don't have better analysis than everyone else. They just have a process they actually follow. Every. Single. Time.

This week I took 5 trades on XAU. All structured, all validated before entry. Not all were perfect but none were impulsive.

That's the only edge that compounds over time.

Sunday question : what's the one rule you keep breaking that you know is costing you money?

comments.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
comments.body:
For me the rule I kept breaking was entering without a structured second check.

So I built a tool that forces it. Upload a chart screenshot, the AI validates the structure before you enter. That's what kept me consistent this week on XAU.

Free to try : [https://herculmarket.com](https://herculmarket.com)

comments.author: Appreciationize818
comments.body: the rule i keep breaking is trading the first 15 minutes after a major news release. know its a bad idea, watch the spread widen, enter anyway because the move looks obvious. lost about 600 on XAUUSD in two separate incidents doing exactly that in the last 6 weeks. not a complex problem, just cant seem to make myself wait
